title: First Conditional: Practice
description: Make sentences with the first conditional.

cards.back: If it rains, I will stay home. (I'll stay home.)
cards.example: If + present simple, will + base verb.

cards.back: If you study hard, you will pass. (you'll pass.)
If she doesn't hurry, she will be late.
cards.example: Negative if clause: doesn't + base verb.

cards.back: I will help you if you ask me. (I'll help you)

cards.front: If they ___ (not come), we ___ (start) without them.
cards.back: If they don't come, we will start without them.

cards.front: ___ you study, you won't pass. (if not)
cards.back: Unless you study, you won't pass.

cards.front: If you finish early, you ___ (can / leave).
cards.back: If you finish early, you can leave.

cards.front: If it rains, we ___ (might / stay) home.
cards.back: If it rains, we might stay home.
cards.example: Might = possibility, less certain than will.

title: If Clause → Result
description: Match the condition with its logical result.

title: Build the Conditional
description: Order to form sentences with first conditional.

sentences.prompt: If it rains, I'll stay home:
sentences.correct: If it rains, I will stay home

sentences.prompt: If you study hard, you will pass:
sentences.correct: If you study hard, you will pass

sentences.prompt: If you don't hurry, you'll be late:
sentences.correct: If they don't hurry, they will be late

sentences.prompt: I will help you if you ask me:
sentences.correct: I will help you if you ask me

sentences.prompt: Unless you study, you will not pass:
sentences.correct: Unless you study, you won't pass

title: Quiz: First Conditional
description: Demonstrate your mastery of the first conditional.

questions.question: "If it ___, I'll stay home."
questions.explanation: If clause = present simple, not will.

questions.question: "If she ___ hurry, she'll be late."
questions.explanation: If clause negative: doesn't + base verb.

questions.question: "___ you study, you won't pass." (= If you don't study)
Which is CORRECT?
options: If it rains, I'll stay home.
If it will rain, I'll stay home.
If it rained, I will stay home.
If it rain, I'll stay home.
questions.explanation: If + present simple, will + base verb.

questions.question: "If it rains, we ___ stay home." (possibility, less sure)

title: Quiz: First Conditional Listening
description: Listen and choose the correct answer.

items.audioText: If + present simple, will + base verb
items.question: What did you hear?
options: If + present simple, will + base verb
Negative if clause: doesn't + base verb

items.question: What did you hear?
options: If + present simple, will + base verb

items.question: What did you hear?
options: If + present simple, will + base verb
Negative if clause: doesn't + base verb

title: Dictation Practice
description: Listen and write what you hear.

items.audioText: If + present simple, will + base verb

title: Pronunciation Practice
description: Listen and repeat the key phrases.

items.phrase: If + present simple, will + base verb
items.hint: Practice saying this phrase aloud — from m6-ch2.
Practice saying this phrase aloud — from m6-ch2.
Practice saying this phrase aloud — from m6-ch2.

items.hint: Practice saying this phrase aloud — from m6-ch2.

title: Build the Sentence
description: Drag words into the correct order.

sentences.prompt: If it rains, I'll stay home:
sentences.correct: If it rains, I will stay home

sentences.prompt: If you study hard, you will pass:
sentences.correct: If you study hard, you will pass

sentences.prompt: If you don't hurry, you'll be late:
sentences.correct: If they don't hurry, they will be late
